title: Linux Setuid Binary Creation
description: Detects creation of setuid/setgid binaries. Attackers set the SUID bit on binaries to maintain root-level access even when running as unprivileged users. This is a classic privilege escalation persistence technique.
query: ('Log Source' = 'SOC Linux Syslog Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Secure Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Syslog Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Audit Logs') and (((msg like '*chmod +s*' or msg like '*chmod u+s*' or msg like '*chmod g+s*' or msg like '*chmod 4755*' or msg like '*chmod 4775*' or msg like '*chmod 2755*' or msg like '*chmod 6755*')))
falsepositives: Package installation setting legitimate SUID bits
